mechanism: All platform-managed capabilities (identity, VNet, monitoring, automation) are in a single `landingzone-resources` resource group within the landing zone subscription.
why: Multiple resource groups split management across boundaries — teardown requires coordinated operations across groups. A central platform subscription creates a remote dependency the landing zone doesn't own.

related.note: deleteAll on this group is a complete teardown of platform infrastructure.
Identity provisioned and protected within the stack.
Single resource group is the stack's deleteAll scope — unmanaged resources removed on every deployment.
Log Analytics workspace provisioned and lifecycle-managed within the stack.
VNet provisioned and lifecycle-managed within the stack.
Container App Jobs provisioned within the stack as the landing-zone-native automation runtime.

violations: Platform resource in the application team's resource group — outside stack scope.
Application resource inside landing-zone-resources — deleted on next stack deployment.
Platform resource created directly in Azure — survives decommission as residual state.
